items.action: Port one-line intent summaries from MDX overview paragraphs to Figma COMPONENT_SET description fields for the 45 primary components. The content already exists in code — this is a copy operation, not content creation.
items.value_framing: Without Figma descriptions, any agent using the Figma API to select or configure components will be operating blind. The content exists in code but is inaccessible to design-side tooling.
items.projected_score_improvement: Cluster 3 from 41.3 to ~60, Overall from 62.8 to ~67

items.action: Add descriptions to Figma variable definitions, prioritising Theme semantics (548 vars) and Colors primitives (135 vars). Use code-side JSDoc comments and naming conventions as the source for descriptions.
items.value_framing: Token descriptions are the primary mechanism for agents to understand token purpose. At 0.3% coverage, agents will misuse tokens by guessing from names alone. The Theme collection (548 tokens across 4 modes) is the highest-impact target — these are consumed by every component.
items.projected_score_improvement: Cluster 1 from 58.8 to ~71, Overall from 62.8 to ~66

items.action: Add YAML frontmatter to MDX component documentation files with structured fields (component_name, purpose, category, status, related_components). Start with the 10 most-used components.
items.value_framing: Without structured metadata, agents must parse prose to extract component purpose and relationships. Frontmatter makes this a single JSON-like lookup.

items.action: Create a parity gap register documenting known architectural differences between Figma variables and code tokens, naming convention mapping, and intentional omissions (motion, grid styles).
items.value_framing: Without a gap register, automated drift detection generates false positives on every run. The 1682 unmatched tokens will appear as new issues each time until the intentional gaps are documented.
items.projected_score_improvement: Cluster 6 from 54.2 to ~71, Overall from 62.8 to ~65

items.action: Create a Motion variable collection in Figma with duration and easing tokens matching the code definitions (6 durations, 6 easing curves). Or document the absence as intentional with a note in the file's Read me page.
items.value_framing: Motion specifications exist in code but are invisible in Figma. Designers working in Figma cannot reference canonical motion values, leading to inconsistent animation timing across products.

items.action: Add empty state variants to data-dependent components (Data Table, Dropdown, List, Select) or create a reusable EmptyState component pattern.
items.value_framing: Without empty state patterns, every team using data-driven components must design their own empty states. This leads to inconsistent experiences and wasted design effort across products.

items.action: Add Error handling and Edge cases sections to MDX documentation for the 10 most-used components. This completes the six-level documentation hierarchy.
items.value_framing: Agents generating UI code from component documentation will produce implementations that handle the happy path but not error or edge cases. This creates correction cycles when QA reveals unhandled states.

items.action: Refactor code-side component tokens (packages/themes/src/component-tokens/) to alias semantic theme tokens rather than hardcoding hex values. This restores the three-layer alias chain.
items.value_framing: Broken alias chains mean that a theme change at the semantic level will not propagate to component tokens. Each component token must be manually updated per theme — a process that does not scale with 4 themes and growing component count.

items.action: Publish tokens in DTCG (Design Token Community Group) format alongside the existing JS exports. This enables standard tooling interop (Style Dictionary, Token Studio) without replacing the current workflow.
items.value_framing: Custom JS format requires Carbon-specific extraction tooling. DTCG format would allow any standard token tool to consume Carbon tokens, reducing integration friction for teams adopting the system.

items.action: Standardise token binding depth across all components to match the Text Input level (colour, typography, spacing, contextual layer variables). Audit and bind under-bound components.
items.value_framing: Inconsistent binding depth means agents will get different quality results depending on which component they inspect. Fully-bound components resolve tokens correctly; under-bound components produce hardcoded values.
